new_classes.label_en: Vermouth and Flavoured Wine of Fresh Grapes (heading 2205)
new_classes.label_de: Wermutwein und aromatisierter Wein aus frischen Weintrauben (Position 2205)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine made from fresh grapes that has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, including vermouth, Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla and Crema di Marsala all'uovo. Distinguished from heading 2204 by the addition of flavouring agents (plants, aromatic substances, egg yolks, almonds, etc.).

new_classes.label_en: Flavouring Process (with plants or aromatic substances)
new_classes.definition_en: A process by which plants, aromatic substances, or other flavouring agents (including egg yolks or almond preparations) are added to wine of fresh grapes to produce a flavoured wine classified under heading 2205.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criterion — that the wine is made from fresh grapes AND has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances — is captured via the new FlavouredWineOfFreshGrapes class (subclass of WineOfFreshGrapes) and the FlavouringProcess restriction. The legal text also enumerates specific product examples (Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, Crema di Marsala all'uovo), which are Marsala-based wines flavoured with egg yolks or almond preparations. These specific named varieties are not individually modelled as distinct subclasses (narrow-over-broad principle), but the FlavouredWineOfFreshGrapes class is broad enough to subsume them. Coverage is slightly reduced because: (1) there is no existing TBox property to positively assert 'isFlavoured' as a boolean quality directly on the wine object — the flavouring is instead captured via the process relation; (2) the specific flavouring agents (egg yolk, almond) could in principle be modelled as part-restrictions but are not, per the narrow-over-broad principle; (3) the complement restriction on WineOfFreshGrapes is used to signal disjointness from unflavoured wine of fresh grapes (heading 2204), though this is expressed as a process complement rather than a direct class disjointness axiom.
